rationale: Both candidates correctly tie the failure to PR #4834's new NotImplementedError guard (matching yenuo26's thread comment), but both then confidently assert the issue is already fixed on main and should be closed — contradicting the actual thread, which shows Flink-ddd still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run to investigate, i.e. no confirmed fix or resolution exists yet. Y compounds this overconfidence with extra unverifiable specifics (a precise PR title, a merge date, three named regression tests with line ranges, an extra RuntimeError guard citation) that appear nowhere in the given context, increasing hallucination risk, whereas X at least hedges that the fix 'appears to already be present' and suggests cherry-picking if not. Neither candidate addresses the maintainers' actual outstanding ask (triggering the CI cluster test).
